steps.title: Spot the self-understanding failure
steps.content: The claim requires evidence that readers haven't understood themselves. The correct response opens with “You have not known what you are”—the defining phrase that directly matches this claim about self-unawareness.

steps.title: Match the quotation to the claim
steps.content:
This phrase, combined with “slumber'd upon yourself” and “eyelids have been the same as closed most of the time,” directly illustrates readers unaware of their own nature.

The other three miss this gap: B and C address unrelated themes (the reader's potential and the speaker's devotion), while D discusses external lack of understanding rather than self-awareness.

This confirms A is correct.
